proved: The public batch request contains the actual question and frozen source objects plus exact remote provider/model routes, per-route ceilings, and a summed total ceiling.
Rust rejects a mismatched total, duplicate routes, duplicate sources, misleading control characters, empty content, excessive routes/sources, and per-route or total budgets above 100.

The native disclosure text lists every route and ceiling, total remote calls, source count, cross-provider model-output sharing, storage/ZDR descriptions, policy dates and URLs, and a 30-minute lifetime without including the question, source label, or source excerpt.
Denial returns no authorization ID and stores no authority.
Approval creates a random 256-bit lowercase-hex capability, a SHA-256 scope identity, and an expiring Rust process-memory record; the active registry is bounded to 64 entries.
The typed status command returns only run/source/route/budget/expiry metadata and the scope digest; explicit revocation removes the capability and is idempotent.
Authorization does not read the OS credential vault or contact a network endpoint.
The compiled MCP reports native-scoped-authorizer-no-product-executor while exposing no provider authorization or generation tool.
notProved: The packaged native authorization dialog has been clicked or visually inspected.
Any model call consumes the authorization; no authorized executor exists.
Route and total budgets decrement atomically under concurrent calls, because consumption is intentionally not implemented.
An authorized call rechecks exact run, question, frozen source content, route, expiry, and revocation before vault or network access.
A product workflow creates, monitors, revokes, or persists an adversarial run.
Live provider behavior, quality, cost, latency, retention, or panel superiority.
